paragraphs: This page groups the current public projects and older technical work.
AgenticCareerBoost is the current workflow and site repository. P3CTeX is LaTeX/document tooling. IronBank is older Java/Spring backend work from the banking simulation project.

items.title: AgenticCareerBoost
items.subtitle: A repo-backed record of agentic development: rules, failures, reports, validation, and public proof.
items.summary: Agentic workflow system, public website, formal reports, state evidence, validation tools, and project-history ledger.

blocks.lead: This is the project behind the site: a public record of agentic engineering work, not just a polished prompt.
paragraphs: AgenticCareerBoost keeps the workflow, the site, the CV, formal LaTeX reports, state logs, validation scripts, and review evidence together. The useful part is that the claims can be inspected in source files, PDFs, tests, and public routes.

The project proves a practical skill: designing model-agnostic agent workflows that can do work, fail visibly, recover through review, and leave enough evidence for a human to understand the result later.

items.value: Authoritative behavior lives in agents/rules: execution modes, truth hierarchy, workflows, roles, and public-copy guardrails.
agents/state records current status, logs, research, summaries, and human-owned external tasks without becoming future law.
agents/reports/tex stores formal LaTeX sources; site/files/reports exposes the human-facing PDFs.
site and the root entrypoints serve the website, dashboard, project routes, CV views, and downloadable artifacts.

items.title: Bootstrap Launch
items.text: S-000 created the first path-based agentic operating system, LaTeX report infrastructure, CI, LinkedIn research, and launch options.

items.title: Initial Growth
items.text: S-001 and S-001.5 turned the system toward positioning, profile cleanup, CV material, recruiter-readiness, and the first public artifacts.

items.title: Context Poisoning
items.text: The system accumulated stale style, duplicated paths, old drafts, over-broad tests, and state/rule confusion that could steer later agents badly.

items.title: End of Semester Pause
items.text: The work paused with useful artifacts present but with campaign momentum and public explanation still uneven.

items.title: Failed Campaign Kickoff
items.text: The attempted restart exposed the poison: profile/social artifacts existed, but the structure and evidence boundaries were not clean enough to launch confidently.

items.title: Cleanup And Current Version
items.text: The current version separates rules from evidence, keeps discarded drafts out of future context, publishes formal reports, validates routes, and keeps S-005 ready for a cleaner social launch.

items.title: Project history map
items.text: Timeline bridge across Bootstrap Launch, Initial Growth, Context Poisoning, pause, failed kickoff, and cleanup.

items.title: Agentic system guide
items.text: Human-facing manual for how to read the repository, roles, workflows, state, and reports.

items.title: S-000 bootstrap case study
items.text: Detailed technical record of the original agentic operating system launch.

items.title: S-001 positioning report
items.text: Portfolio audit, market research, positioning synthesis, and initial profile direction.

items.title: S-001.5R system review
items.text: Remediation report for S-001.5 publication governance and missing documentation.

items.title: S-002R restart refresh
items.text: Restart review across framework, site foundation, GitHub profile evidence, LinkedIn reactivation, and repo-local closure.

items.title: S-003 website clarity
items.text: Public route-map cleanup, project-page translation, metadata surfaces, browser checks, and deferred site work.

items.title: S-004 documentation alignment
items.text: Career guardrail, relaunch calibration, human-owned account gates, and the correction away from generic market drift.

items.title: S-004.5 site quality
items.text: Generated status, runtime metadata, dashboard rendering, responsive fixes, legal disclosure, and browser-render validation.

items.title: Refactor retrospective
items.text: Failure analysis for process overgrowth, context poisoning, evidence retention, and cleanup architecture.

items.title: Evidence reconciliation
items.text: Local review findings, intentional draft deletion, route fixes, and validation ledger.

paragraphs: The current version is intentionally smaller and sharper. Direct user scope controls execution mode. Stable rules live in agents/rules. Evidence lives in agents/state. Formal reports live in agents/reports/tex and are published as PDFs under site/files/reports.

The website is the curated public mirror, not the authority source. It should explain the project clearly enough for a recruiter or peer to understand the arc, then point to the PDFs and source files for proof.

External publication, LinkedIn account changes, outreach, and applications remain human-owned. The repository can prepare evidence and drafts, but it does not pretend that public human actions are already done.

items.title: P3CTeX
items.subtitle: LaTeX tooling for reports, diagrams, listings, CV material, and repeatable document builds.
items.summary: LaTeX work for reports, CV material, diagrams, listings, screenshots, and document builds.

paragraphs: P3CTeX is my LaTeX class and package work for academic and technical documents.
It handles page structure, localized academic labels, code listings, screenshots, diagrams, covers, headers, report conventions, and CV/report builds.
The related p3cTeX-UMLST work extracts UML and listing behavior into a smaller package.

items.title: IronBank
items.subtitle: Java/Spring Boot banking simulation.
items.summary: Java/Spring Boot banking simulation from the Ironhack period.

paragraphs: IronBank is older backend work from my Ironhack period. It belongs here as part of the backend and banking-domain route.
The project covers accounts, users, transactions, permissions, services, and persistence.
Today I would document more, test more, and make the domain rules more explicit, but the project still marks the Java/Spring baseline.
